chapters.title: Anyone can look stylish with well-fitting clothes
chapters.text:
There is a thought in the mainstream that says to be fashionable; you need to be tall and skinny. This fallacy is damaging the confidence of millions of people. The truth is that you don’t need to have any specific body type to hit the fashion high notes. You just need to wear clothes that suit you and fit you well.

Alison Freer has years of experience in the fashion industry. She has dressed every type of body shape out there, and she says with complete conviction that the only enemy you have facing you when it comes to being stylish is choosing clothes that don’t tailor to your body correctly.

When clothes gape, bag, or pull too tight on your body, they aren’t the right fit for you. Look for tailored clothes which compliment your body and make you feel great.

Looking good all the time sounds impossible; we all have our bad days, after all. However, if you choose clothes that go with your personal style, flatter your shape, and sit perfectly, there’s no reason why you can’t be that person who always looks wholly put together. “How to Get Dressed” doesn’t dictate to you what clothes you should wear and which you should throw in the bin; it educates you on why you can wear anything you want, as long as you like it, wear it with confidence, and it fits you correctly.

Far too many people buy clothes from the store and assume that they will suit them if they choose their specific size. That’s not true. Every person is a slightly different shape, even within a size, so how can a pair of pants, a skirt, a T-shirt, or a blouse fit perfectly for everyone? Becoming downhearted because a pair of pants doesn’t work perfectly off the rack will lead to body confidence issues. Instead, learn how to tailor your clothes to fit you to perfection. That way, you’ll never wonder how to be stylish again.

It’s a total lie that you need a certain body type to be truly stylish. ~ Alison Freer

chapters.title: A good tailor can be the difference between stylish and a fashion mistake
chapters.text:
If it’s not that common to pull clothes off the rack and have them fit you perfectly, how are you supposed to find items that flatter your body? With the services of a quality tailor, that’s how.

Most clothes can be altered to fit you well, and that’s the difference between having a wardrobe full of clothes that make you feel amazing and a closet full of clothes that do nothing for your self-esteem.

Ask around to find the best tailor in your local area. Social media is also a good option, as you can read reviews. Once you’ve found the best of the best, keep their number in your phone.

Your clothes should perform well no matter what you’re doing. For instance, if a blouse pulls across the chest when you move, it’s not performing its best and doing what it’s supposed to do. If a pair of pants bags in the groin area when you sit down, they do not fit you well. A good quality tailor can fix these problems very quickly. It’s entirely possible to learn how to do this yourself, but unless you have a sewing machine and all the expert knowledge, it’s much easier to give it to a professional!

However, sometimes it’s just not worth trying to fix clothing. Anything too small needs to be gifted to someone who will wear it. A tailor can add extra fabric, but it’s not going to sit as it should, and you’re going to notice odd lines as you wear it.

It’s no fun when an item of clothing is too small for you. Make yourself feel better by donating it to a charity store — a good deed will perk your confidence.

Anything with intricate detailing is also best avoided when it comes to altering it. Sequins can quickly come unfastened and fall all over the floor whenever you move in a specific direction. Lace can easily tear and appear to be of poor quality, and chiffon needs to avoid altering to keep it looking as loose and flowing as it’s designed to be. In those cases, you simply need to give the item as a gift and find something that suits you better.

Did you know? The origins of tailoring can be traced back to the Middle Ages. During these times, tailors designed padded items to be worn under chainmail armor.

chapters.title: Your signature style may change over time, but it’s important to know what it is
chapters.text:
Everyone has their own signature style. When you look at Hollywood stars like Johnny Depp, Helena Bonham Carter, and Lady Gaga, these are all individuals who dress in a way that pleases them. Still, it suits them perfectly because it sums up their personality. It’s essential to try and find your style and be aware that everyone’s personal style changes a little over time.

You can’t copy someone else’s style. Focus on yourself. What makes you comfortable, and what do you like?

The key to finding your personal style is to know yourself well. Alison Freer suggests sitting down with a notebook and pen and doing a spot of brainstorming. What do you like? What are you passionate about? What keywords sum up your personality? What is your vision for life? What makes you happy, and what makes you sad? Ask some searching questions to create a picture of who you are as a person. As you do this, an image of your style will start to form in your mind. Perhaps you’ll lean towards darker tones, or maybe you’re all about bright colors.

As you start to recognize your personal style, keep it in mind when you’re dressing and heading out shopping. Don’t try and force yourself to fit into a specific box; just because leopard print is in fashion, that doesn’t mean you have to wear it if it just doesn’t fit in with your style and preference. Lady Gaga follows her style, no matter what is in fashion at the time.

Your personal style is how you see yourself, and it transmits out to how the world sees you. Dressing with your personality in mind makes fashion more fun!

As you grow older and your preferences and hobbies grow, be sure to keep tweaking your personal style. It’s not a rigid view that you have to hold throughout your life; everyone changes and style needs to evolve alongside it.

Once you’ve established what you’re looking for, be on the lookout for it everywhere, even in the most unlikely places. ~ Alison Freer

chapters.title: So-called fashion rules are made to be broken
chapters.text:
Throughout the history of fashion, there have always been “rules.” Alison Freer argues that, in truth, rules don’t exist, and they’re made to be broken.

One particular rule says that you shouldn’t wear stripes. If you wear horizontal lines and your body is a little curvier, it’s supposed to make you look bigger. If you wear vertical stripes and you’re taller, you’re going to seem longer. These guidelines are all rubbish! You can wear any stripe you want; you simply need to look in a full-length mirror and see how you feel about it. Try playing around with the thickness of lines or go for a pinstripe and see how that looks. Everyone is different, and fashion rules cannot incorporate every single person on the planet.

Fashion is designed to be playful and fun. There is no space for rules in a situation that is supposed to bring you joy. Throw away the rulebook and enjoy dressing yourself.

Another fallacy is that you can’t wear anything black if you’re already wearing brown or navy blue. Why? Brown and black look great together, as long as the brown shade is a little lighter. The same goes for navy blue; there are different shades of every single color, and you just need to experiment with them.

Play around with jewelry metals and ignore the “rule” that you have to only stick to gold or silver. What about rose gold? That’s a great middle-of-the-road option, and you can mix that with anything you like. Stack up different colored bangles and play around with tones. All of this will help you to find your personal style and avoid falling into pigeon-holed boxes that do nothing for your sense of personality or style.

Another rule that Alison Freer doesn’t subscribe to is that you can’t wear leggings as pants. Provided you choose thicker material leggings and they aren’t sheer around the backside, there is no reason whatsoever that you can’t wear these as pants!

When wearing leggings as pants, a good tip is to take a flash photo of your backside before you go out. Just check there is no sheerness to the leggings; if not, you’re good to go.

chapters.title: Storing your clothes properly will make them last longer
chapters.text:
How you store your clothes matters. If you throw everything into your closet in a haphazard manner, you’re going to end up wearing the same things all the time, simply because you can’t actually see the clothing you own. You’re also going to end up creasing everything, and it’s not going to last as long as it could have.

Alison Freer suggests maximizing your hanging space because hanging up your clothing is the best option. When you hang your clothes, you can easily see what you have, everything appears more organized, and it keeps your clothes fresher. Of course, hanging also means that your clothes aren’t likely to crease as much.

You don’t need to spend a small fortune on storage boxes or a walk-in wardrobe; you just need to learn how to maximize the space you have.

If you’re rotating your clothes by season, find a large suitcase and set layers of paper tissue down between your clothes. Fold everything carefully and lay it flat. It would be best if you then stored the suitcase under the bed, at the bottom of the closet, or even on top. Once you’re ready to rotate your clothes again, wash everything to freshen it up.

A few other helpful storage hacks to keep your clothes and accessories fresh and organized include: 1. Use plastic laundry baskets to store handbags and arrange them side by side so you can easily see them. 2. Use hanging shoe racks and place them on the inside of cupboard doors. 3. Use slim hangers with fabric/velvet flocking to stop clothes from falling off or snagging on the hangers. 4. Use hangers that have separate compartments hanging down. This way, you can use one hanger for several items, e.g., maxi dresses, long skirts, pants, etc. 5. Line your drawers with cutlery trays and use the compartments for your jewelry. 6. Arrange your shoes on your bookshelves.

Organizing your clothing and accessories means you can see what you have. You can mix and match your items much easier when you know exactly what is sitting inside your wardrobe.

chapters.title: Vintage shopping is difficult but certainly not impossible
chapters.text:
One area of shopping that many people seem to struggle with is vintage shopping. That’s probably because vintage shopping is far more complicated than it looks, and it requires a little knowledge into whether the item you’re buying is indeed worth the price tag or not.

There are several places you can shop for vintage items. Alison Freer suggests looking in thrift and charity stores, as you can often find some gems within the piles of items. You may need to dig, especially if you’re in a thrift store, but you’ll find bargains, for sure.

Vintage items are not only old; they’re also pieces that were fashionable and in demand at the time. Every era has a specific style that sums it up. What period are you looking for?

Another possible spot is a consignment shop. These are stores that effectively act as an agent for someone who wants to sell their old items. The seller will earn around 50 to 60%, and the consignment store will keep the rest of the sale amount. You’ll often find high-end goods as well as vintage items in consignment shops. You won’t see significant bargains, but you will discover some designer items for lower prices than if you bought them new.

It’s worth remembering that authentic vintage items aren’t going to be cheap. The lowest you can expect to pay is around $50 for something small, and anywhere up to about $1,000 from there. Some stores are happy to take your cash and sell you an item that isn’t vintage at all; it simply looks old. For that reason, you need to do your research and know what you’re looking for.

Be prepared to pay more for vintage items but understand that they’re an investment. You can always sell the item and often gain a profit from what you originally paid.

Alison Freer suggests wearing comfortable clothing and flat shoes when you’re on the hunt for vintage items, as you need to dedicate the whole day to it, and you’re going to be walking a lot. Read online before you head out and know the era you’re looking for, the popular labels at the time, as well as the on-trend styles.

Once you find an item, check it for any damage and try it on. Have a very close eye on the item, as a small hole could easily stretch and damage it beyond repair. Vintage items can be altered, but to keep the style and value, it’s best to avoid it if you can.

chapters.title: Conclusion
chapters.text:
True style is about knowing who you are, what you want, how you want to look, and what suits you best. It’s effortless to just go along with fashion fads and follow the masses, but how is that helping you to identify your own personal style?

Many people take a lifetime to find their style, while others manage to understand it within a few years. Know that your style will change, and be okay with that. It’s fun to evolve and try new things constantly, and your clothes should reflect your changing tastes and values.

We live in a fast-fashion world. Items are on-trend for five minutes, and then they’re cast aside, never to be seen again. When you dress for your own style, you avoid wasting money on fads, and you’re always going to look your best because you dress well. It’s also important to find clothes that fit you correctly, and if they don’t fit when you buy them, take them to a professional tailor to have them altered to fit you perfectly.

Copying someone else’s style is just going to make you feel uncomfortable. If you want to be genuinely at ease in your clothes, you need to find your style and make sure that everything you have in your wardrobe fits you well. Throw out those baggy pants that you wear around the house, and give those shirts that are too tight and do nothing for you. It’s better to have fewer items that fit you well than a huge closet full of pieces that pull, bag, stretch, and gape.

Try this

It’s time for a spring clean. Open your closet and pull everything out. Create a tailor pile and a gifting pile out of the items that don’t fit you well.

How much money do you spend per month on clothing? Sit down and work out a budget. It’s best to invest in your wardrobe than to simply spend too much on pieces that aren’t your style or fit.

What items have you always wanted to try but always been scared to attempt? Be brave and explore.

Read it till the end?
